title: Tour of my Solar Power system in my van - Request

transcript.text: Here is a request video on my solar system on my van. Here's my van. I'll do an
Organizational video later. So here's my solar panel. It's one feet by four
Feet and so we drill the hole in there and the cable goes in there and passes
Pretty soon. Passes into here over to that battery. That's a regular $100 like RV
Battery, deep cycle battery. So the cable that comes from that solar panel goes to
Here and then we have a little the little positive wire goes to the positive and
Negative goes to the negative. So when you get the battery you have to get the one
That has the the thing that takes the little guys to but all you needed to do
I mean actually you don't have to have that kind of battery because you just
Get the little attachment on the top. It goes on the top and then that cable goes
Into the wall. We just took this panel off. My friend helped me do this and and then
Actually I'm sorry and then the cable goes to this guy. The inverter which a YouTube subscriber gave to me.
This is awesome about a year and a half ago. Yeah this is just like a simple like hundred dollar inverter.
I should try to have it turned off. I have it turned off now so that if it if it just kind of sits around it it'll drain it even if it's sunny out so just have it turned off unless you're using it.
And then it's good to charge the battery too. Like with a battery charger? You have one?

Okay then it goes into the wall there. And then I just have a normal outlet that it attaches to. Here's my little panel. It says how many you know voltages and amps. It has a little fuse there so if it stops working I just have to get another one of those but they sell those all over the place.

Now when I get around to it the battery of the car is underneath that seat but you also have a little area underneath this seat that can hold a battery and so when I get around to it I'll put another battery there just normal car battery there and then have it attached to the whole system back here and then have it attached also to the main car battery with a little switch.

So when I stop the car. So when I stop the car I just switch it off so that doesn't suck all the battery the energy out of all of these and when I am but when I'm driving it the alternator of the car can help charge that battery and then the RV battery so it doesn't rely on just the solar panel and

And let's see and then maybe I'll get like a I heard about these these like wind propellers that you can put on your car that when you drive down the road they spin around and it can charge a battery too or I could just put another solar panel up there because I have more room up there.

But with that one solar panel it's enough to charge a battery like if you use your computer this this RV battery can charge power the computer maybe eight hours or something before the little inverter beeps which means that it's low and then you just turn it off.
And then it would on a sunny day like this it would take maybe three days of a sunny day like this to charge it back up enough to be able to use the computer again.
So you could use a computer every day just from when it charges on the solar panel maybe I don't know half hour or something maybe an hour.

I didn't really have that much time to check check it out because I wasn't hanging out in my van living in my van all the time until I was in Seattle and then it was in Seattle it was just like overcast all the time so and then the car engine exploded so.

But the whole system I got this like in 97 so I think the panel was like $100 the inverter was $1,000 back then but now they're only like $100 and then the rest of the system you know maybe another $100 so yeah it's not too expensive definitely worth having.

I mean cars should come with them stock with I mean every car should have it but the technology these days is you can just plug something into the cigarette lighter you know and I think those can power computers too.
I haven't experimented with that but then you're using up your car battery so yeah these kinds of vans and cars like this should come stock with the solar panel and all that kind of stuff.

But it's definitely worth having and before I live in my van again whenever that is I'll get another car battery put in there and have it hooked up to the whole the whole kit and caboodle and then I can then I can really be hanging out in my van.
Just park in front of like a library or Starbucks and then I can be plugged in all day long and I don't have to rely on anybody and this car is pretty comfortable.
Pretty comfortable to just hang out and hang out with somebody else there or put your legs up there.
Okay so that's that.
Peace out.
